facts.text: The applicant, Mr Murat Arslan, is a Turkish national who was born in 1979 and is currently detained in Nazilli Prison (Turkey).

anchor.surface_text: The applicant, Mr Murat Arslan, is a Turkish national who was born in 1979 and is currently detained in Nazilli Prison (Turkey). He was represented before the Court by Mr E. Yildiz, a lawyer practising in Izmir.

facts.text: The applicant was represented before the Court by Mr E. Yildiz, a lawyer practising in Izmir.

anchor.surface_text: The applicant, Mr Murat Arslan, is a Turkish national who was born in 1979 and is currently detained in Nazilli Prison (Turkey). He was represented before the Court by Mr E. Yildiz, a lawyer practising in Izmir.

facts.text: On 9 October 2001 the applicant was arrested and taken into police custody at the headquarters of the anti-terrorism branch of the Izmir security police.

anchor.surface_text: On 9 October 2001 the applicant was arrested and taken into police custody at the headquarters of the anti-terrorism branch of the Izmir security police.

facts.text: On 12 October 2001, after being interviewed by the public prosecutor at the Izmir National Security Court, the applicant was taken before a judge of that court who on 13 October 2001 ordered his detention pending trial.

anchor.surface_text: On 12 October 2001, after being interviewed by the public prosecutor at the Izmir National Security Court, he was taken before a judge of that court who on 13 October 2001 ordered his detention pending trial.

facts.text: On 13 October 2001 a judge of the Izmir National Security Court ordered the applicant's detention pending trial.

anchor.surface_text: On 12 October 2001, after being interviewed by the public prosecutor at the Izmir National Security Court, he was taken before a judge of that court who on 13 October 2001 ordered his detention pending trial.

facts.text: On 19 October 2001 the public prosecutor committed the applicant for trial in the National Security Court.

anchor.surface_text: On 19 October 2001 the public prosecutor committed the applicant for trial in the National Security Court.

facts.text: The criminal proceedings against the applicant are still pending.

anchor.surface_text: The criminal proceedings against the applicant are still pending.

facts.text: The applicant’s lawyer dated his application 12 April 2002 and took it on 19 April to the post office in Konak (central Izmir), where post is collected regularly several times a day.

anchor.surface_text: The applicant’s lawyer dated his application 12 April 2002 and took it on 19 April to the post office in Konak (central Izmir), where post is collected regularly several times a day.

facts.text: The applicant’s lawyer took the application to the post office in Konak on 19 April 2002.

anchor.surface_text: The applicant’s lawyer dated his application 12 April 2002 and took it on 19 April to the post office in Konak (central Izmir), where post is collected regularly several times a day.

facts.text: The applicant’s detention in police custody ended on 13 October 2001.

anchor.surface_text: The Court notes that the applicant’s detention in police custody ended on 13 October 2001, the date on which the judge of the National Security Court ordered his detention pending trial.

facts.text: The applicant’s lawyer dated the letter accompanying the application 12 April 2002.

anchor.surface_text: In that connection, the Court notes that the applicant’s lawyer dated the letter accompanying the application 12 April 2002, but posted it at the post office in Konak (central Izmir) on 19 April.

facts.text: The applicant’s lawyer posted the application at the post office in Konak on 19 April 2002.

anchor.surface_text: In that connection, the Court notes that the applicant’s lawyer dated the letter accompanying the application 12 April 2002, but posted it at the post office in Konak (central Izmir) on 19 April.

facts.text: The applicant has given no explanation of the six day interval between the date on which the letter was written and that on which it was posted.

anchor.surface_text: The Court observes that the applicant has given no explanation of the six day interval between the date on which the letter was written and that on which it was posted.

facts.text: The Court considers that the application was lodged on 19 April 2002.

anchor.surface_text: Having regard to the above considerations, the Court considers that the application was lodged on the date it was posted, namely 19 April 2002.

anchor.surface_text: six-month rule. It refers on that point to the close correlation between the two rules (see, for example, De Becker v. Belgium, Commission decision of 9 June 1958, Yearbook 2, p. 243). The Court reiterates that if no remedies are available in domestic law the six months begin to run in principle from the date of the act complained of in the application, or on the date when the applicant first became aware of it.
